info.title: walt.id verifier
info.description:
Interact with the walt.id verifier. Version is reported to be [latest] and this service instance was started 2026-01-13T00:46:22Z.
Questions about anything here? Visit support.

post.description: Initializes an OIDC presentation session, with the given presentation definition and parameters. The URL returned can be rendered as QR code for the holder wallet to scan, or called directly on the holder if the wallet base URL is given.

parameters.name: authorizeBaseUrl
parameters.description: Base URL of wallet authorize endpoint, defaults to: openid4vp://authorize

parameters.name: responseMode
parameters.description: Response mode, for vp_token response, defaults to direct_post

parameters.name: successRedirectUri
parameters.description: Redirect URI to return when all policies passed. "$id" will be replaced with the session id.

parameters.name: errorRedirectUri
parameters.description: Redirect URI to return when a policy failed. "$id" will be replaced with the session id.

parameters.name: statusCallbackUri
parameters.description: Callback to push state changes of the presentation process to

parameters.name: openId4VPProfile
parameters.description: Optional header to set the profile of the VP request, available profiles: DEFAULT, ISO_18013_7_MDOC, EBSIV3, HAIP

parameters.name: sessionTtl
parameters.description: Optional header to set the sessionTtl of the VP request, in seconds

requestBody.description: Presentation definition, describing the presentation requirement for this verification session. ID of the presentation definition is automatically assigned randomly.

200.description: URL for the holder wallet to continue verification

post.summary: Verify vp_token response, for a verification request identified by the state
post.description: Called in direct_post response mode by the SIOP provider (holder wallet) with the verifiable presentation in the vp_token and the presentation_submission parameter, describing the submitted presentation. The presentation session is identified by the given state parameter.

parameters.name: state
parameters.description: State, i.e. session ID, identifying the presentation session, this response belongs to.

get.summary: Get info about OIDC presentation session, that was previously initialized
get.description: Session info, containing current state and result information about an ongoing OIDC presentation session

get.summary: Retrieve decoded credentials associated with a successfully verified presentation session
get.description:
Returns a structured, verbose representation of all credentials presented
in a successfully verified presentation session. This endpoint is only available
for sessions whose `vp_token` was verified with a positive result (`verificationResult == true`).

Credentials are grouped by format and returned in both decoded and raw forms.

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: The identifier of the presentation session whose credentials should be retrieved.

parameters.name: viewMode
parameters.description: Optional parameter controlling how detailed the response will be (defaults to `simple`).

achievement.name: JFF x vc-edu PlugFest 3 Interoperability
achievement.description: This wallet supports the use of W3C Verifiable Credentials and has demonstrated interoperability during the presentation request workflow during JFF x VC-EDU PlugFest 3.

criteria.narrative: Wallet solutions providers earned this badge by demonstrating interoperability during the presentation request workflow. This includes successfully receiving a presentation request, allowing the holder to select at least two types of verifiable credentials to create a verifiable presentation, returning the presentation to the requestor, and passing verification of the presentation and the included credentials.

get.summary: Get presentation definition object by ID
get.description: Gets a presentation definition object, previously uploaded during initialization of OIDC presentation session.

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: ID of presentation definition object to retrieve

get.summary: Get request object for session by session id
get.description: Gets the signed request object for the session given by the session id parameter

Authorize endpoint of OAuth Server as defined in EBSI Conformance Testing specifications. 
Response is a 302 redirect with VP_TOKEN or ID_TOKEN request. 
Use the /oidc4vp/verify endpoint using the header openId4VPProfile to get an EBSI-compliant VP_TOKEN request without redirects.
